site.title: Miles "Tails" Prower Multiverse
site.tagline: A clean guide to Tails across games, animation, comics, and film.

variants.name: Tails (Original Sonic Series)
variants.bio: The core version of Miles Prower is Sonic's closest ally and a genius mechanic despite his young age. He combines bravery with a gentle personality, often acting as the team's engineer and pilot. Across the games, he grows from sidekick to dependable hero in his own right.
traits: Mechanical prodigy; builds aircraft, gadgets, and support tech
Can fly by spinning his twin tails like a rotor
Usually optimistic and kind, but capable under pressure
Long-term best friend and tactical partner to Sonic
variants.appearanceDiff: Bright orange fur with white muzzle/chest and large expressive eyes; this look is the baseline for most adaptations. Proportions and shoe/glove details change over generations, but this is the reference design most versions branch from.
variants.media: Mainline and side Sonic games by SEGA (Genesis era to modern releases including Sonic Frontiers and beyond).

variants.name: Tails (Sonic X)
variants.bio: In Sonic X, Tails keeps his inventor identity but gets extra emotional focus and character-driven episodes. He is thoughtful, sensitive, and loyal, often balancing science-minded logic with deep empathy. His friendship with Cosmo in later arcs gives this version one of the franchise's most heartfelt storylines.
traits: More introspective and emotionally vulnerable than many game portrayals
Frequent pilot and technical support for interdimensional problems
Strong teamwork dynamic with Cream, Amy, and Sonic
Key role in Metarex arc with high emotional stakes
variants.appearanceDiff: Very close to game design, but anime linework and softer facial styling make him appear younger and more delicate in expression-heavy scenes.
variants.media: TV anime adaptation by TMS Entertainment spanning Earth arc and Metarex arc.

variants.name: Tails (Sonic Movie Trilogy)
variants.imageAlt: Sonic the Hedgehog 2 movie poster featuring Tails
variants.bio: Movie Tails is introduced as a capable young fox who has looked up to Sonic from afar. He arrives with gadgets, tactical know-how, and a sincere desire to help, quickly becoming family rather than just a sidekick. This portrayal leans into his sweetness while giving him cinematic action moments.
traits: High-tech gadget user with compact cinematic gear
Warm, earnest personality with a fanboy-to-family arc
Keeps pace in action set pieces despite smaller size
Strong trio chemistry with Sonic and Knuckles
variants.appearanceDiff: More textured fur and realistic rendering to fit live-action environments; facial proportions are tuned for expressive CGI realism while preserving his iconic orange-white palette.
variants.media: Paramount Pictures Sonic film universe (notably Sonic the Hedgehog 2 and onward references in trilogy continuity).

variants.name: Tails (IDW Comics)
variants.imageAlt: Cover of Sonic the Hedgehog #1 from IDW
variants.bio: IDW's Tails expands on his post-war confidence arc and technical leadership after major game events. He remains gentle and principled, but is written with sharper initiative and field competence. The comics highlight his role in crisis response, science strategy, and moral center moments.
traits: Leads engineering and logistics under sustained conflict
Balances compassion with practical decision-making
Works closely with Restoration teams and allies
Maintains continuity-minded growth from modern games
variants.appearanceDiff: Visual design stays close to modern game canon, with comic inking and color choices giving stronger contrast and dynamic motion during action panels.
variants.media: IDW Publishing's ongoing Sonic comic line, generally aligned with game continuity tone and events.

variants.name: Nine (Sonic Prime)
variants.bio: Nine is an alternate-dimensional Tails shaped by isolation and survival in New Yoke City. Unlike mainline Tails, he is distrustful, guarded, and intensely independent, channeling his intellect into defensive technology. His relationship with Sonic becomes one of the most morally complex dynamics in the series.
traits: Uses nine mechanical tails as modular weapons/tools
More cynical and self-protective than classic Tails
Exceptionally inventive under harsh circumstances
Driven by autonomy and control over his own world
variants.appearanceDiff: Darker tone with extra cybernetic tail rig and edgier silhouette; visual language emphasizes tech augmentation over cheerful softness.
variants.media: Netflix animated series Sonic Prime, focused on multiverse variants created by shattered realities.

variants.name: Tails (Sonic Boom)
variants.bio: Boom Tails is a more overtly gadget-driven team tech specialist with a lighter comedic tone. He often acts as mission control and invention source, translating problems into practical tools. The writing gives him playful banter while keeping his heart and loyalty intact.
traits: Engineer-first characterization with frequent inventions
Comedic timing and ensemble sitcom chemistry
Acts as support strategist and builder for the team
Retains the classic friendly, polite personality core
variants.appearanceDiff: Wears utility accessories and sports/athletic styling common to Boom redesigns; overall look is more adventure-gear oriented than main game canon.
variants.media: Sonic Boom TV series and companion games in a separate continuity.

variants.name: Tails (Archie Comics)
variants.bio: Archie Tails evolves through one of the franchise's longest-running alternate continuities. Depending on era, he is portrayed as a younger sidekick, maturing adventurer, and occasionally central figure in high-concept comic arcs. This version is notable for broad world-building and unique character relationships.
traits: Large continuity range with substantial long-term growth
Participates in lore-heavy arcs and alternate timelines
Often more serialized drama than game portrayals
Keeps core intelligence and loyalty across reboots
variants.appearanceDiff: Art style changes heavily by artist and era; design can shift from classic rounded proportions to more angular, action-comic silhouettes.
variants.media: Archie Comics' long-running Sonic series and spin-offs prior to IDW licensing era.
